title: Case study: ablation across feature groups
scope: Honest reporting of what helped. Demonstrates a practical workflow combining the concepts of baseline, ablation, and selection across diverse sets of constructed features.
key_concepts: Walks through a hypothetical prediction scenario (e.g., credit default risk).
Shows establishing a baseline model, then testing separate feature blocks (demographics, transaction history, app interaction logs).
Demonstrates making a final architectural decision by weighing predictive lift against computational cost and stability.
examples: Dropping transaction history causes the accuracy to plummet (keep it). Dropping app interaction logs barely changes accuracy but significantly lowers latency (drop it).
relations_to_other_chapters: Synthesizes the concepts of Ch 9 into an applied decision-making process.

provenance.source: Synthetic case study designed for this curriculum.
